prose:
Method–provenance summary (required fields)

• Property: atom-condensed Fukui functions (f⁺ electrophilic, f⁻ nucleophilic, f⁰, dual descriptor) and identification of the most electrophilic and most nucleophilic sites for aniline; the headline numerical value reported is the f⁺ (electrophilic) Fukui index of the most electrophilic atom.
• Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier “standard”), density-fitted RI-J as employed in the PySCF backend.
• Geometry provenance: single-point analyses on the fixed user-supplied geometry at /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz; no re-optimization (notation: B3LYP/def2-TZVP//input_xyz).
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model).
• Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (0,1); cation (+1,2); anion (–1,2).
• Software: PySCF 2.x invoked via chemkit “fukui-reactivity” skill.
• Convergence & caveats: SCF converged for all charge states.  Engine integrity status: ok; trustworthy = true.

Headline result
Most electrophilic site: atom index 2 (1-based label 3, C) with f⁺ = 2.2975867.

Reproducibility
The run command (from `cli_invocation`) was:
/…/cli.py fukui --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz --out …live.json
Live engine output was streamed to the associated .out log (path displayed at job launch) and is available for inspection.

All warnings from the tool (VERBATIM):
1. sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
2. sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
3. common header present

No additional warnings were reported.
